zh.title: 雪崩币/美元技术分析：短线偏弱，仍处于震荡下探阶段
zh.content:
AVAX/USD当前报6.1451，较24小时回落1.62%，价格运行在20日均线6.30118下方，说明短线仍偏弱。近期高点6.2515未能有效突破，反弹力度不足，市场更像是弱势整理而非趋势反转。

MACD为-0.0120且仍在零轴下方，空头动能尚未完全释放；RSI缺失，需结合价格位置判断，当前更关注量能是否放大。若持续站稳6.09附近，才有望缓和下行压力并形成短线筑底。

下方支撑先看6.09一线，若失守，可能进一步回测6.00整数位及前低区域；上方阻力在6.25附近，其次是6.30的20日均线和6.35区域。只有重新收复6.30，短线结构才会改善。

操作上宜以反弹减仓、回踩观察为主，不宜在未确认止跌前追多。激进交易者可等6.09附近企稳后轻仓试多，止损设在6.00下方；保守者则等待放量突破6.30再跟进。

en.title: Avalanche vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
AVAX/USD is showing a short-term bearish-to-neutral structure. The latest price at 6.1451 sits below the 20-day SMA of 6.30118, which suggests the market is still trading under near-term trend pressure. The 24-hour decline of 1.62% also points to weak momentum, although the relatively tight high-low range between 6.2515 and 6.0894 indicates sellers have not yet triggered a sharp breakdown.

Indicator signals lean cautious. The MACD reading of -0.0120 is slightly negative, implying momentum remains soft and the pair is vulnerable to further drift if buyers do not step in soon. RSI is unavailable, so overbought or oversold conditions cannot be confirmed, which reduces confidence in any aggressive momentum trade. In this environment, price action around support and resistance becomes especially important.

Immediate support is near 6.09, the current session low, followed by the psychological 6.00 level, which may attract buyers if tested. If that floor gives way, downside could accelerate as stop-loss orders are triggered. On the upside, initial resistance sits at 6.25, then the 20-day SMA near 6.30. A stronger recovery would likely need a sustained move above 6.30 to improve the short-term outlook.

For traders, a cautious approach is reasonable. Conservative buyers may prefer to wait for a clean reclaim of 6.30 before entering, since that would signal improving momentum. More active traders could consider small entries near 6.09 to 6.00 with tight risk control below support. If price repeatedly fails near 6.25 to 6.30, rallies may be better treated as short-term selling opportunities rather than trend reversals.
